heading: Multiple Fields from Documents
body:
> **Example:** You want to write 176 MiB of data as BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) files to an Azure (Microsoft Azure) Blob Storage
> container named `my-container`.
>
> Using the following $out syntax:
>
> ```json
> {
>   "$out": {
>     "azure": {
>       "serviceURL": "http://mystorageaccount.blob.core.windows.net/",
>       "container": "my-container",
>       "region": "eastus2",
>       "filename": {
>         "$concat": [
>           "persons/",
>           "$name", "/",
>           "$uniqueId", "/"
>         ]
>       },
>       "format": {
>         "name": "bson",
>         "maxFileSize": "200MiB"
>       }
>     }
>   }
> }
> ```
>
> $out writes 176 MiB of data to BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) files. To name each
> file, $out concatenates:
>
> - A constant string `persons/` and, from the documents:
>
>   - The string value of the `name` field,
>
>   - A forward slash (`/`),
>
>   - The string value of the `uniqueId` field, and
>
>   - A forward slash (`/`).
>
> Each BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) file contains all of the documents with the same
> `name` and `uniqueId` values. $out names each file using
> the documents' `name` and `uniqueId` values.
